description: Key Nigerian government officials from 2010 to present (April 2026)

sources.note: Cross-reference with official government sources for exact dates

presidents.name: Goodluck Ebele Jonathan
presidents.note: Assumed office after death of President Umaru Musa Yar'Adua on 5 May 2010. Previously served as Acting President from 9 February 2010.

presidents.name: Muhammadu Buhari
presidents.note: First opposition candidate to win a presidential election in Nigeria's history. Served two terms.

presidents.name: Bola Ahmed Tinubu
presidents.note: Former Governor of Lagos State (1999-2007). Incumbent president.

vicePresidents.name: Namadi Sambo
vicePresidents.note: Became Vice President when Jonathan assumed presidency. Previously Governor of Kaduna State.

vicePresidents.name: Yemi Osinbajo
vicePresidents.note: Professor of Law. Served as Acting President during Buhari's medical leaves in 2017.

vicePresidents.name: Kashim Shettima
vicePresidents.note: Former Governor of Borno State (2011-2019). Incumbent vice president.

senatePresidents.name: David Mark
senatePresidents.note: Served two terms as Senate President spanning the 6th and 7th National Assemblies.

senatePresidents.name: Bukola Saraki
senatePresidents.note: Elected Senate President of the 8th National Assembly. His election was controversial as he was not the APC leadership's preferred candidate.

senatePresidents.name: Ahmad Ibrahim Lawan
senatePresidents.note: Senate President of the 9th National Assembly.

senatePresidents.name: Godswill Akpabio
senatePresidents.note: Former Governor of Akwa Ibom State (2007-2015). Senate President of the 10th National Assembly. Incumbent.

speakers.name: Dimeji Bankole
speakers.note: Speaker of the 6th House of Representatives. Served until end of the assembly term.

speakers.name: Aminu Waziri Tambuwal
speakers.note: Speaker of the 7th House of Representatives. Later defected to APC and became Governor of Sokoto State.

speakers.name: Yakubu Dogara
speakers.note: Speaker of the 8th House of Representatives.

speakers.name: Femi Gbajabiamila
speakers.note: Speaker of the 9th House of Representatives. Later appointed Chief of Staff to President Tinubu.

speakers.name: Tajudeen Abbas
speakers.note: Speaker of the 10th House of Representatives. Incumbent.

financeMinsters.name: Olusegun Olutoyin Aganga
financeMinsters.note: Served under Jonathan as Minister of Finance. Later moved to Minister of Trade and Investment.

financeMinsters.name: Ngozi Okonjo-Iweala
financeMinsters.note: Served as Coordinating Minister for the Economy and Minister of Finance. Previously held the same role under Obasanjo (2003-2006). Later became Director-General of the WTO.

financeMinsters.name: Kemi Adeosun
financeMinsters.note: First Finance Minister under Buhari. Resigned over NYSC certificate controversy.

financeMinsters.name: Zainab Shamsuna Ahmed
financeMinsters.note: Served as Minister of Finance, Budget and National Planning. Held the position until the end of the Buhari administration.

financeMinsters.name: Wale Edun
financeMinsters.note: Appointed Minister of Finance and Coordinating Minister of the Economy under Tinubu. Incumbent.

administrations.name: Jonathan Administration
keyEvents: Assumed office after Yar'Adua's death (May 2010)
Independence Day bombing in Abuja (Oct 2010)
Post-election violence kills ~800 (April 2011)
Partial fuel subsidy removal sparks Occupy Nigeria protests (Jan 2012)
Boko Haram insurgency escalates — state of emergency in 3 NE states (2013)
Nigeria rebased as Africa's largest economy (April 2014)
Chibok schoolgirls kidnapping — 276 taken (April 2014)
#BringBackOurGirls global movement
Successful Ebola outbreak containment (2014)
Power sector privatisation — PHCN broken into 15 firms
Lost election to Buhari — first democratic transfer to opposition (2015)

administrations.name: Buhari Administration (First Term)
administrations.speaker: Yakubu Dogara
keyEvents: Declared Boko Haram 'technically defeated' (Dec 2015)
Economy enters recession — GDP contracts (2016)
Naira devalued and floated for the first time (June 2016)
Extended medical leave in London — VP Osinbajo acts as president (2017)
Anti-corruption drive: 603 convictions by EFCC by 2018
Farmer-herder clashes kill thousands in Middle Belt (2017-2018)
Finance Minister Kemi Adeosun resigns over NYSC certificate scandal (Sept 2018)
Banditry surges in northwest — Zamfara, Katsina, Kaduna

administrations.name: Buhari Administration (Second Term)
administrations.speaker: Femi Gbajabiamila
keyEvents: COVID-19 pandemic — economy enters second recession (-3.62% Q3 2020)
#EndSARS protests and Lekki toll gate shooting (Oct 2020)
Twitter banned for 7 months (June 2021 - Jan 2022)
Boko Haram leader Shekau killed (May 2021)
Mass school kidnappings escalate — Kaduna, Niger, Zamfara (2021)
Owo church massacre — 40+ killed (June 2022)
Kuje prison break — inmates freed by insurgents (July 2022)
Kaduna-Abuja train kidnapping (March 2022)
Naira redesign cash crisis (2022-2023)
Lekki Deep Sea Port commissioned (Jan 2023)

administrations.name: Tinubu Administration
administrations.speaker: Tajudeen Abbas
keyEvents: Fuel subsidy removed on inauguration day — 'Subsidy is gone' (May 2023)
Naira floated again — crashes from ₦460 to ₦750+ overnight (June 2023)
Supreme Court upholds contested election result (Oct 2023)
Petrol prices surge from ₦65 to ₦537-617 per litre (2023)
Naira hits all-time low of ₦1,717/USD (Nov 2024)
NBS reveals 2.2 million kidnappings in 12 months (Dec 2024)
Cost of living crisis — nationwide #EndBadGovernance protests (Aug 2024)
National anthem changed back to 1960 version (May 2024)
9,662 killed in conflict in 2024 — worst year on record (ACLED)
St. Mary's school kidnapping — 303 children taken (Nov 2025)
6,800 killed and 5,400 kidnapped in H1 2025 alone
Coup plot uncovered — 16 military officers arrested (Sept 2025)
U.S. military strikes ISIS in NW Nigeria at Tinubu's request (Dec 2025)
Jos Palm Sunday massacre — 28+ killed (Mar 2026)
₦68.3T budget approved for 2026 — largest in history

nationalAssemblies.speaker: Dimeji Bankole
nationalAssemblies.note: Overlaps with start of Jonathan presidency

nationalAssemblies.speaker: Yakubu Dogara
nationalAssemblies.note: Buhari first term. Notable friction between executive and legislature.

nationalAssemblies.speaker: Femi Gbajabiamila
nationalAssemblies.note: Buhari second term. Period of #EndSARS protests (October 2020).
